author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 37–47.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our Lord, in the form of a servant, yet not a servant, but even in servant-form the Lord (for that form of flesh was indeed servant-like; but though He was “in the likeness of sinful flesh,” yet was He not sinful flesh) promised freedom to those who believed in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Jews, as if proudly glorying in their own freedom, refused with indignation to be made free, when they were the servants of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And therefore they said that they were free, because Abraham’s seed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What answer, then, the Lord gave them to this, we have heard in the reading of this day’s lesson.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I know,” He said, “that ye are Abraham’s children; but ye seek to kill me, because my word taketh no hold in you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I recognize you, He says; “Ye are the children of Abraham, but ye seek to kill me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I recognize the fleshly origin, not the believing heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye are the children of Abraham,” but after the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He says, “Ye seek to kill me, because my word taketh no hold in you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If my word were taken, it would take hold: if ye were taken, ye would be enclosed like fishes within the meshes of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then means that—“taketh no hold in you”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It taketh not hold of your heart, because not received by your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so is the word of God, and so it ought to be to believers, as a hook to the fish: it takes when it is taken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No injury is done to those who are taken; since they are taken for salvation, and not for destruction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence the Lord says to His disciples: “Come after me, and I shall make you fishers of men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But such were not these; and yet they were the children of Abraham,—children of a man of God, unrighteous themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they inherited the fleshly genus, but were become degenerate, by not imitating the faith of him whose children they were.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard, indeed, the Lord saying, “I know that ye are Abraham’s children.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what He says afterwards: “I speak that which I have seen with my Father; and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had already said, “I know that ye are Abraham’s children.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it, then, that they do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What He told them: “Ye seek to kill me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This they never saw with Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord wishes God the Father to be understood when He says, “I speak that which I have seen with my Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have seen the truth: I speak the truth, because I am the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Lord speaks the truth which He has seen with the Father, He has seen Himself—He speaks Himself; because He Himself is the Truth of the Father, which He saw with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He is the Word—the Word which was with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The evil, then, which these men do, and which the Lord chides and reprehends, where have they seen it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With their father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we come to hear in what follows the still clearer statement who is their father, then shall we understand what kind of things they saw with such a father; for as yet He names not their father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A little above He referred to Abraham, but in regard to their fleshly origin, not their similarity of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is about to speak of that other father of theirs, who neither begat them nor created them to be men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But still they were his children in as far as they were evil, not in as far as they were men; in what they imitated him, and not as created by him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They answered and said unto Him, Abraham is our father;” as if, What hast thou to say against Abraham? or, If thou canst, dare to find fault with Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that the Lord dared not find fault with Abraham; but Abraham was not one to be found fault with by the Lord, but rather approved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these men seemed to challenge Him to say some evil of Abraham, and so to have some occasion for doing what they purposed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Abraham is our father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear how the Lord answered them, praising Abraham to their condemnation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto them, If ye are Abraham’s children, do the works of Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, he was praised, they were condemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham was no manslayer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say not, He implies, I am Abraham’s Lord; though did I say it, I would say the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He said in another place, “Before Abraham was, I am” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: 58); and then they sought to stone Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But meanwhile, as you see me, as you look upon me, as alone you think of me, I am a man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, then, wish you to kill a man who is telling you what he has heard of God, but because you are not the children of Abraham?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet He said above, “I know that ye are Abraham’s children.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not deny their origin, but condemns their deeds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Their flesh was from him, but not their life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we, dearly beloved, do we come of Abraham’s race, or was Abraham in any sense our father according to the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh of the Jews draws its origin from his flesh, not so the flesh of Christians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have come of other nations, and yet, by imitating him, we have become the children of Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the apostle: “To Abraham and to his seed were the promises made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith not,” he adds, “And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed, and heirs according to the promise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We then have become Abraham’s seed by the grace of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not of Abraham’s flesh that God made any co-heirs with him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He disinherited the former, He adopted the latter; and from that olive tree whose root is in the patriarchs, He cut off the proud natural branches, and engrafted the lowly wild olive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, when the Jews came to John to be baptized, he broke out upon them, and addressed them, “O generation of vipers.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Very greatly indeed did they boast of the loftiness of their origin, but he called them a generation of vipers,—not even of human beings, but of vipers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saw the form of men, but detected the poison.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet they had come to be changed, because at all events to be baptized; and he said to them, “O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bring forth therefore fruits meet for repentance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And think not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father; for God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If ye bring not forth fruits meet for repentance, flatter not yourselves about such a lineage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God is able to condemn you, without defrauding Abraham of children.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He has a way to raise up children to Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who imitate his faith shall be made his children.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such are we.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In our parents we were stones, when we worshipped stones for our god.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of such stones God has created a family to Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, does this empty and vain bragging exalt itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them cease boasting that they are the children of Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They have heard what they ought to have heard: “If ye are the children of Abraham,” prove it by your deeds, not by words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye seek to kill me, a man;”—I say not, meanwhile, the Son of God; I say not God; I say not the Word, for the Word dies not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say merely this that you see; for only what you see can you kill, and whom you see not can you offend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This,” then, “did not Abraham.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye do the works of your father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as yet He says not who is that father of theirs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now what answer did they give Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they began somewhat to realize that the Lord was not speaking of carnal generation, but of their manner of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because it is the custom of the Scriptures, which they read, to call it, in a spiritual sense, fornication, when the soul is, as it were, prostituted by subjection to many false gods, they made this reply: “Then said they to Him, We be not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham has now lost his importance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they were repulsed as they ought to have been by the truth-speaking mouth; because such was Abraham, whose deeds they failed to imitate, and yet gloried in his lineage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they altered their reply, saying, I believe, with themselves, As often as we name Abraham, he goes on to say to us, Why do ye not imitate him in whose lineage ye glory?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such a man, so holy, just, and guileless, we cannot imitate.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us call God our Father, and see what he will say to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has falsehood indeed found something to say, and should not truth find its fitting reply?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear what they say: let us hear what they hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We have one Father,” they say, “even God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said Jesus unto them, If God were your Father, ye would [doubtless] love me; for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but He sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye call God Father; recognize me, then, as at least a brother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At the same time He gave a stimulus to the hearts of the intelligent, by touching on that which He has a habit of saying, “I came not of myself: He sent me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I proceeded forth and came from God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Remember what we are wont to say: From Him He came; and from whom He came, with Him He came.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The sending of Christ, therefore, is His incarnation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as respects the proceeding forth of the Word from God, it is an eternal procession.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Time holds not Him by whom time was created.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no one be saying in his heart, Before the Word was, how did God exist?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Never say, Before the Word of God was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God was never without the Word, because the Word is abiding, not transient; God, not a sound; by whom the heaven and earth were made, and which passed not away with those things that were made upon the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From Him, then, He proceeded forth as God, the equal, the only Son, the Word of the Father; and came to us, for the Word was made flesh that He might dwell among us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His coming indicates His humanity; His abiding, His divinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is His Godhead towards which, His humanity whereby, we make progress.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had He not become that whereby we might advance, we should never attain to Him who abideth ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Why,” He says, “do ye not understand my speech?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even because ye cannot hear my word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so they could not understand, because they could not hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence could they not hear, but just because they refused to be set right by believing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye are of your father the devil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How long do ye keep speaking of a father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How often will ye change your fathers,—at one time Abraham, at another God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear from the Son of God whose children ye be: “Ye are of your father the devil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, now, we must beware of the heresy of the Manicheans, which affirms that there is a certain principle of evil, and a certain family of darkness with its princes, which had the presumption to fight against God; but that God, not to let His kingdom be subdued by the hostile family, despatched against them, as it were, His own offspring, princes of His own [kingdom of] light; and so subdued that race from which the devil derives his origin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From thence, also, they say our flesh derives its origin, and accordingly think the Lord said, “Ye are of your father the devil,” because they were evil, as it were, by nature, deriving their origin from the opposing family of darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So they err, so their eyes are blinded, so they make themselves the family of darkness, by believing a falsehood against Him who created them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For every nature is good; but man’s nature has been corrupted by an evil will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What God made cannot be evil, if man were not [a cause of] evil to himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But surely the Creator is Creator, and the creature a creature [a thing created].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The creature cannot be put on a level with the Creator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Distinguish between Him who made, and that which He made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The bench cannot be put on a level with the mechanic, nor the pillar with its builder; and yet the mechanic, though he made the bench, did not himself create the wood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord our God, in His omnipotence and by the Word, made what He made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had no materials out of which to make all that He made, and yet He made it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they were made because He willed it, they were made because He said it; but the things made cannot be compared with the Maker.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou seekest a proper subject of comparison, turn thy mind to the only-begotten Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, were the Jews the children of the devil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By imitation, not by birth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the usual language of the Holy Scriptures.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The prophet says to those very Jews, “Thy father was an Amorite, and thy mother a Hittite.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Amorites were not a nation that gave origin to the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Hittites also were themselves of a nation altogether different from the race of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because the Amorites and Hittites were impious, and the Jews imitated their impieties, they found parents for themselves, not of whom they were born, but in whose damnation they should share, because following their customs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps you inquire, Whence is the devil himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the same source certainly as the other angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the other angels continued in their obedi ence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, by disobedience and pride, fell as an angel, and became a devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But listen now to what the Lord says: “Ye,” said He, “are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is how ye are his children, because such are your lusts, not because ye are born of him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are his lusts?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was a murderer from the beginning.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This it is that explains, “the lusts of your father ye will do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye seek to kill me, a man that telleth you the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, too, had ill-will to man, and slew man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the devil, in his ill-will to man, assuming the guise of a serpent, spoke to the woman, and from the woman instilled his poison into the man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They died by listening to the devil, whom they would not have listened to had they but listened to the Lord; for man, having his place between Him who created and him who was fallen, ought to have obeyed the Creator, not the deceiver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “he was a murderer from the beginning.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at the kind of murder, brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The devil is called a murderer not as armed with a sword, or girded with steel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came to man, sowed his evil suggestions, and slew him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think not, then, that thou art not a murderer when thou persuadest thy brother to evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou persuadest thy brother to evil, thou slayest him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to let thee know that thou slayest him, listen to the psalm: “The sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye, then, “will do the lusts of your father;” and so ye go madly after the flesh, because ye cannot go after the spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was a murderer from the beginning;” at least in the case of the first of mankind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the very time that murder [manslaughter] could possibly be committed, he was a murderer [manslayer].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Only from the time that man was made could manslaughter be committed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For man could not be slain unless man was previously made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “he was a murderer from the beginning.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence a murderer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he stood [abode] not in the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he was in the truth, and fell by not standing in it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why “stood he not in the truth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because the truth is not in him;” not as in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In such a way is the truth [in Him], that Christ Himself is the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, he had stood in the truth, he would have stood in Christ; but “he abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard the words of the Gospel: you have received them with attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here now, I repeat them, that you may clearly understand the subject of your thoughts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord said those things of the devil which ought to have been said of the devil by the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That “he was a murderer from the beginning” is true, for he slew the first man; “and he abode not in the truth,” for he lapsed from the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When he speaketh a lie,” to wit, the devil himself, “he speaketh of his own;” for he is a liar, and its [his] father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From these words some have thought that the devil has a father, and have inquired who was the father of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Indeed this detestable error of the Manicheans has found means down to this present time wherewith to deceive the simple.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they are wont to say, Suppose that the devil was an angel, and fell; and with him sin began as you say; but, Who was his father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We, on the contrary, reply, Who of us ever said that the devil had a father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they, on the other hand, rejoin, The Lord saith, and the Gospel declares, speaking of the devil, “He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and his father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear and understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I shall not send thee far away [for the meaning]; understand it from the words themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord called the devil the father of falsehood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what it is, only revolve the words themselves, and understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not every one who tells a lie that is the father of his lie.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou hast got a lie from another, and uttered it, thou indeed hast lied in giving utterance to the lie; but thou art not the father of that lie, because thou hast got it from another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the devil was a liar of himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He begat his own falsehood; he heard it from no one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As God the Father begat as His Son the Truth, so the devil, having fallen, begat falsehood as his son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearing this, recall now and reflect upon the words of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye catholic minds, consider what ye have heard; attend to what He says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The devil—“was a murderer from the beginning.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We admit it,—he slew Adam.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he abode not in the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We admit it, for he lapsed from the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because there is no truth in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: True: by falling away from the truth he has lost its possession.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is both a liar, and the father of lies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou, it may be, art a liar, because thou utterest a lie; but thou art not its father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou hast got what thou sayest from the devil, and hast believed the devil, thou art a liar, but not the father of the lie.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he, because he got not elsewhere the lie wherewith in serpent-form he slew man as if by poison, is the father of lies just as God is Father of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Withdraw, then, from the father of lies: make haste to the Father of truth; embrace the truth, that you may enter into liberty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those Jews, then, spake what they saw with their father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what was that but falsehood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord saw with His Father what He should speak; and what was that, but Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, but the Word of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, but the truth of the Father, eternal itself, and co-eternal with the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, then, “was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him; when he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own, for he is a liar,”—and not only a liar, but also “the father of it;” that is, of the very lie that he speaks he is the father, for he himself begat his lie.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which of you convicteth me of sin,” as I convict both you and your father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If I say the truth, why do ye not believe me,” but just because ye are the children of the devil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that is of God heareth God’s words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, again, it is not of their nature as men, but of their depravity, that you are to think.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this way they are of God, and yet not of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By nature they are of God, in depravity they are not of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, I pray you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the gospel you have the remedy against the poisonous and impious errors of the heretics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of these words also the Manicheans are accustomed to say, See, here there are two natures,—the one good and the other bad; the Lord says it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What says the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye therefore hear me not, because ye are not of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what the Lord says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, he rejoins, dost thou say to that?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what I say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are both of God, and not of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By nature they are of God: by depravity they are not of God; for the good nature which is of God sinned voluntarily by believing the persuasive words of the devil, and was corrupted; and so it is seeking a physician, because no longer in health.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is what I say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou thinkest it impossible that they should be of God, and yet not of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear why it is not impossible.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are of God, and yet not of God, in the same way as they are the children of Abraham, and yet not the children of Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you have it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not as you say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearken to the Lord Himself; it is He that said to them, “I know that ye are the children of Abraham.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Could there be any lie with the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then is it true what the Lord said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then it is true that they were the children of Abraham?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But listen to Himself denying it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who said, “Ye are the children of Abraham,” Himself denied that they were the children of Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If ye are Abraham’s children, do the deeds of Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now ye seek to kill me, a man that telleth you the truth, which I have heard from God: this did not Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye do the works of your father,” that is, of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, were they both Abraham’s children, and yet not his children?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both states He showed in them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were both Abraham’s children in their carnal origin, and not his children in the sin of following the persuasion of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, also, apply it to our Lord and God, that they were both of Him, and not of Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How were they of Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He it was that created the man of whom they were born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How were they of Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He is the Architect of nature,—Himself the Creator of flesh and spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, were they not of Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because they had made themselves depraved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were no longer of Him, because, imitating the devil, they had become the children of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore came the Lord God to man as a sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast heard the two names, both man and sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As man, he is of God; as a sinner, he is not of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the moral evil in man be distinguished from his nature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let that nature be owned, to the praise of the Creator; let the evil be acknowledged, that the physician may be called in to its cure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord then said, “He that is of God heareth the words of God: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not distinguish the value of different natures, or find, beyond their own soul and body, any nature in men which had not been vitiated by sin; but foreknowing those who should yet believe, them He called of God, because yet to be born again of God by the adoption of regeneration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To these apply the words “He that is of God heareth the words of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that which follows, “Ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God,” was said to those who were not only corrupted by sin (for this evil was common to all), but also foreknown as those who would not believe with the faith that alone could deliver them from the bondage of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On this account He foreknew that those to whom He so spake would continue in that which they derived from the devil, that is, in their sins, and would die in the impiety in which they resembled him; and would not come to the regeneration wherein they would be the children of God, that is, be born of the God by whom they were created as men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In accordance with this predestinating purpose did the Lord speak; and not that He had found any man amongst them who either by regeneration was already of God, or by nature was no longer of God.
